title: Complimenting food
description: Dinner at a friend's home — warm up the host with genuine Cantonese compliments
location: A friend's home, Sham Shui Po
context: Being invited to a Hong Kong home for a meal is an honour. Hosts will insist you eat more even when you're full — 食多啲 is the default hospitality phrase. Complimenting the food directly and asking who cooked it shows real appreciation.
culturalFact: Saying 你過獎喇 (you're too kind / you flatter me) is the standard humble response when someone compliments your cooking. If you don't say it, the host might think you're not being polite!

lines.speaker: host
lines.jyutping: sik6 do1 di1! m4 gau3 gaa3!

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: hou2 sik6 aa3! hai6 nei5 zing2 gaa3?
lines.english: This is delicious! Did you make it yourself?

lines.speaker: host
lines.cjk: 係呀，呢個係我阿媽教落嚟㗎。
lines.jyutping: hai6 aa3, ni1 go3 hai6 ngo5 aa3 maa1 gaau3 lok6 lei4 gaa3.
lines.english: Yes, this recipe was passed down from my mum.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 咁滋味！我從來冇食過咁好食嘅魚。
lines.jyutping: gam3 zi1 mei6! ngo5 cung4 loi4 mou5 sik6 gwo3 gam3 hou2 sik6 ge3 jyu2.
lines.english: It's so flavourful! I've never had fish this good before.

lines.speaker: host
lines.jyutping: m4 hai6 aa3, nei5 gwo3 zoeng2 laa3!
lines.english: Oh stop it, you're too kind!

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 真係，下次可唔可以教我煮？
lines.jyutping: zan1 hai6, haa6 ci3 ho2 m4 ho2 ji5 gaau3 ngo5 zyu2?
lines.english: I mean it — could you teach me to cook it next time?

lines.speaker: host
lines.jyutping: gang2 hai6 dak1! fun1 jing4 nei5 ceoi4 si4 lei4.
lines.english: Of course! You're welcome any time.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 今晚真係好開心，多謝你款待。
lines.jyutping: gam1 maan5 zan1 hai6 hou2 hoi1 sam1, do1 ze6 nei5 fun2 doi6.
lines.english: Tonight has been really lovely — thank you for having me.
